notes: This file is owned by Agent 11 only.
Structure nests rounds inside sprints to keep history organized.

sprint_meta.notes: Perf sprint. Agent 11 owns regression tests (Wave 1) and final QA gate (Wave 6).

round_meta.build_target: Regression tests targeting POST-FIX behavior (tests SHOULD fail until Agent 03 lands Wave 2). Covers WK58-BUG-001, WK58-BUG-002, WK58-BUG-003 plus a placeholder for Phase-3 batching that is not yet implemented.

wk58_w1_test_tooling_and_regression.prompt_summary: Author tests/test_terrain_perf.py per Section 3 / Tool 2 of the Ursina Entities Overload master plan. TestTerrainEntityTracking with three active tests (one per WK58 bug) plus TestTerrainChunkBatching with one skipped Phase-3 test. Each docstring names the bug it validates. Confirm pytest fails 1-3 times for the expected reasons and that the rest of the suite still passes.

what_i_changed.summary: Created tests/test_terrain_perf.py (4 tests: 3 active + 1 skipped). Tests use FakeEntity / FakeRenderer / FakeWorld / FakeCamera stand-ins plus targeted unittest.mock.patch so neither Ursina init nor real GLB assets are required. All 3 active tests fail with bug-id-tagged assertion messages, as intended.

commands_run.notes: 3 failed, 1 skipped, 1 warning in 1.48s. All 3 failures map to the expected WK58 bug repros.
332 passed, 1 warning in 39.60s. Existing suite unaffected by the new file.

determinism_guard PASS; pytest FAIL (3 failed, 332 passed, 1 skipped). qa_smoke short-circuits after pytest fails so the observe_sync profiles did not run. The 3 failures are ENTIRELY the new intentional WK58 regression tests; no existing test regressed.

evidence.pytest_summary:
3 failed, 1 skipped, 1 warning in 1.48s

FAILED tests/test_terrain_perf.py::TestTerrainEntityTracking::test_path_entities_are_tracked
  -> AssertionError: Path stone entities are not registered in _visibility_gated_terrain_by_tile. Missing PATH tile keys: [(0,5),(1,5),(2,5),(3,5),(4,5),(5,5),(6,5),(7,5),(8,5),(9,5)] (WK58-BUG-003)

FAILED tests/test_terrain_perf.py::TestTerrainEntityTracking::test_culling_reapplies_after_fog_change
  -> AssertionError: WK58-BUG-001: after /revealmap re-enabled all entities, the next chunk-cull pass with a stationary camera must re-hide entities outside the visible rect. Entities still enabled at out-of-view tiles: [(20,20),(22,22)].

FAILED tests/test_terrain_perf.py::TestTerrainEntityTracking::test_visible_rect_reasonable_at_default_camera
  -> AssertionError: WK58-BUG-002: visible tile rect at default camera (cam_y=70) covers 240/256 chunks (93.8%), well over the <50% target. Returned rect: (0, 0, 249, 226).

SKIPPED tests/test_terrain_perf.py::TestTerrainChunkBatching::test_static_entity_count_reduced (Phase 3 batching not yet implemented)

expected_failures_mapping.test_path_entities_are_tracked: WK58-BUG-003 (path stones bypass visibility tracking). Assertion confirms PATH tile keys absent from _visibility_gated_terrain_by_tile.
expected_failures_mapping.test_culling_reapplies_after_fog_change: WK58-BUG-001 (culling/visibility composition failure). Assertion confirms out-of-rect entities remain enabled after /revealmap when the camera is stationary.
expected_failures_mapping.test_visible_rect_reasonable_at_default_: WK58-BUG-002 (camera visible rect covers ~88% of map). Assertion confirms 93.8% chunk coverage at cam_y=70 with the current view_radius heuristic.
expected_failures_mapping.test_static_entity_count_reduced: Skipped pending Phase 3 / WK58-FEAT-001 static batching.

evidence.qa_smoke_result: FAIL (rc=1) - pytest short-circuit due to the new intentional failures. CRITICAL: all 332 existing tests still pass. Verified via separate run: `python -m pytest tests/ --ignore=tests/test_terrain_perf.py -q` returned exit_code=0 with 332 passed. The qa_smoke FAIL is the consequence of the new regression tests, NOT a regression in existing coverage. Expected to flip back to PASS after Wave 2 fixes land.
notes: Tests use a pure-logic + fakes pattern: no Ursina window, no real GLB asset loading. Heavy dependencies (Entity, _finalize_kenney_scatter_entity, _environment_*_lists, _building_occupied_tiles, _build_terrain_ground_mesh, _set_static_prop_fog_tint) are patched via unittest.mock.patch.object so only the in-scope code path runs.

test_path_entities_are_tracked drives build_3d_terrain end-to-end on a 10x10 world with one horizontal row of PATH tiles. The PATH branch at lines 552-563 of ursina_terrain_fog_collab.py constructs the entity but never calls self.track_visibility_gated_terrain(path_ent, tx, ty). One-line fix per Phase 1B in the master plan.

test_culling_reapplies_after_fog_change reproduces the four step sequence (initial fog sync -> camera cull to one chunk -> /revealmap -> stationary cull). Fails today because cull_terrain_chunks only processes the became_hidden / became_visible sets, which are both empty when the chunk set is unchanged. Phase 1 composition fix (ent._ks_fog_visible && ent._ks_chunk_visible) is the intended remedy.

test_visible_rect_reasonable_at_default_camera patches game.graphics.ursina_renderer.camera with a FakeCamera at cam_y=70 above map center, calls _get_visible_tile_rect on a stub self (the method only uses module-level globals). Returned rect (0,0,249,226) covers 240/256 chunks = 93.8%. Phase 2 frustum tightening must drop this to <50%.

test_static_entity_count_reduced is gated with pytest.mark.skip(reason=...) and the skip body raises AssertionError so it cannot silently pass once unmarked. Re-enable after Phase 3 _batch_static_terrain_for_chunks lands.

qa_smoke FAIL is expected per the master plan (Section 3, Tool 2): 'These tests will initially FAIL (they test for the fixed behavior). That is intentional.' Once Agent 03 lands Phase 1 + 2 in Wave 2, all 3 active tests should turn green and qa_smoke should return to PASS.

follow_ups: After Wave 2 (Agent 03 Phase 1 + 2), Agent 11 must re-run `python -m pytest tests/test_terrain_perf.py -v` and confirm all 3 active tests pass. Then re-run `python tools/qa_smoke.py --quick` to confirm DONE: PASS.

After the conditional Wave 4 / Phase 3 batching lands, Agent 11 must replace pytest.mark.skip on TestTerrainChunkBatching::test_static_entity_count_reduced with the real implementation per the docstring TODO (build terrain with patched Entity, call _batch_static_terrain_for_chunks, assert len(_visibility_gated_terrain) drops to hundreds, assert trees retain _ks_tree_base_scale).

Consider extending test_visible_rect_reasonable_at_default_camera with two more parametrized cases (cam_y=40 zoomed-in, cam_y=100 zoomed-out) once Phase 2 lands, to guard against regressions across the typical zoom band.

round_meta.build_target: Add ONE regression test in tests/test_terrain_perf.py asserting that ensure_fog_overlay early-outs when fog_revision == _fog_revision_seen even when _fog_entity is None (heightmap shader path). Test must FAIL pre-Fix-1.A and PASS post-Fix-1.A.

wk58_w6_real_bottleneck_fixes.prompt_summary: Wave 5 (Agent 10) found ensure_fog_overlay's early-out gate broke on the WK53 heightmap path: gate required _fog_entity is not None, but WK53 destroyed _fog_entity and switched to a shader uniform on _terrain_ground_entity. Result: 62,500-tile loop + GPU upload ran every frame, costing 19.0ms p50. Agent 11 adds tests/test_terrain_perf.py::TestFogOverlayPerf::test_ensure_fog_overlay_early_out_post_heightmap to lock the invariant. Agent 03 ships Fix 1.A in parallel.

what_i_changed.summary: Appended TestFogOverlayPerf class with one test (test_ensure_fog_overlay_early_out_post_heightmap) plus two minimal fakes (_FakeFogTexture, _FakeTerrainGroundEntity). Test constructs the post-WK53 steady-state shape (heightmap active, _terrain_ground_entity set, _fog_entity=None, matched _fog_revision_seen) and asserts that across two ensure_fog_overlay calls with the same fog_revision, pygame.image.frombuffer (the loop body's terminal call before GPU upload) is invoked AT MOST ONCE. Under the buggy pre-fix gate, both calls run the rebuild body -> 2 invocations -> FAIL. Under Fix 1.A, both calls early-out -> 0 invocations -> PASS.
new_helpers: _FakeFogTexture (minimal stand-in for the Ursina Texture returned by TerrainTextureBridge.refresh_surface_texture, exposes .filtering attr only)

_FakeTerrainGroundEntity (empty class — the heightmap branch wraps its setShaderInput in try/except so the AttributeError is silently absorbed, matching production behaviour on entities without a bound shader)
implementation_notes: Spy points: pygame.image.frombuffer (line 269) and TerrainTextureBridge.refresh_surface_texture (line 272). Both are reached only AFTER the early-out gate (lines 213-229). Spy_frombuffer returns a real small pygame.Surface so downstream code that touches surf doesn't crash. Spy_refresh returns _FakeFogTexture so the heightmap branch (lines 295-354) can proceed without real Ursina Texture init.
Re-used existing _FakeEntity / _FakeRenderer / _FakeWorld helpers from earlier tests in the file. world.heightmap is set to a small 2D list to trigger the heightmap branch at line 295.
Lane discipline observed: only tests/test_terrain_perf.py modified; no edits to game/, ai/, tools/, config.py, main.py, or requirements.txt.

commands_run.notes: 1 passed in 1.61s. Test runs against the working-tree (post-fix) code -> PASS. Agent 03's Fix 1.A is already present in the working tree at lines 213-229 of game/graphics/ursina_terrain_fog_collab.py (gate now reads `_fog_entity is not None or terrain_ground_ent is not None`).
5 passed, 1 warning in 1.57s. Matches PM expectation '4 from Wave 1 + 1 new from Agent 11 = 5 active'.

commands_run.cmd: python -c "<simulated old-gate harness; details in evidence.pre_fix_test_result>"
commands_run.notes: Ran an in-memory harness that monkey-patched UrsinaTerrainFogCollab.ensure_fog_overlay to use the OLD (committed HEAD) gate `if fog_revision == my_rev and _fog_entity is not None`. With our test scenario (_fog_entity=None, matched revision), the old gate falls through and the rebuild body runs on BOTH calls -> frombuffer_calls=2 -> assertion `<= 1` fails. Confirms the test discriminates correctly between pre-fix and post-fix gate semantics.

DONE: PASS. determinism_guard PASS, all observe_sync profiles exit 0 (base, intent_bounty, hero_stuck_repro, no-enemies, mock-LLM, speed_scaling 0.1/0.25/0.5/1.0, interior_view, quest_panel, conversation, direct_prompt_integration).

evidence.pre_fix_test_result: SIMULATED FAIL (confirmed). Methodology: ran an in-memory harness that monkey-patched UrsinaTerrainFogCollab.ensure_fog_overlay to mirror the committed-HEAD pre-fix gate (`if fog_revision == my_rev and _fog_entity is not None: return`). With the test's heightmap-active scenario (_fog_entity=None, matched _fog_revision_seen), the old gate fell through and the rebuild body executed on BOTH consecutive matched-revision calls -> pygame.image.frombuffer was invoked 2x -> primary assertion `frombuffer_calls <= 1` would have failed. Direct pre-fix run against committed game/ code was not performed because the task constraint forbids modifying or reverting game/ code in this lane; the in-memory simulation produces the same logical outcome and is reproducible from the test file plus a tiny harness snippet. The discriminator was further verified via boolean algebra: OLD gate `(rev_match) AND (_fog_entity is not None)` evaluates `True AND False = False` -> no early-out; FIX gate `(rev_match) AND (_fog_entity is not None OR _terrain_ground_entity is not None)` evaluates `True AND (False OR True) = True` -> early-out.
evidence.post_fix_test_result: PASS. Working-tree version of game/graphics/ursina_terrain_fog_collab.py:213-229 already contains Agent 03's Fix 1.A (the heightmap-aware gate). Test passes deterministically: frombuffer_calls=0 across two matched-revision calls (both early-out immediately at the gate). Single-test run + full-file run both green.
evidence.full_suite_after: 5 passed, 1 warning in 1.57s. Active tests now: TestTerrainEntityTracking::test_path_entities_are_tracked, TestTerrainEntityTracking::test_culling_reapplies_after_fog_change, TestTerrainEntityTracking::test_visible_rect_reasonable_at_default_camera, TestTerrainChunkBatching::test_static_entity_count_reduced, TestFogOverlayPerf::test_ensure_fog_overlay_early_out_post_heightmap.
evidence.qa_smoke_result: PASS (rc=0). Full output: 'DONE: PASS'. All headless profiles green, determinism guard clean, no regressions.
evidence.log_validated: yes (python -m json.tool ran clean after edit)
evidence.agent03_fix_status: Agent 03's Fix 1.A is present in the working tree (uncommitted). Lines 213-229 of game/graphics/ursina_terrain_fog_collab.py now read: `if int(fog_revision) == my_rev and (self._r._fog_entity is not None or terrain_ground_ent is not None): return`. The comment block at lines 214-224 explicitly tags this as 'WK58 W6 Fix 1.A (Agent 03)'. Agent 11's test locks the invariant against future regressions.
notes: Test uses unittest.mock.patch.object to spy on tfc.pygame.image.frombuffer and tfc.TerrainTextureBridge.refresh_surface_texture without touching the production code. This keeps the test purely observational.

Pre-fix demonstration was performed via an in-process monkey-patch harness rather than reverting game/ code, to honour the file-lane constraint. The simulation is bit-for-bit equivalent to the OLD gate logic and confirms the test would have failed against the committed (pre-Wave-6) code. Full reproducer recipe is preserved in commands_run[2] for future audit.

If a future change to ensure_fog_overlay re-introduces the bug (e.g., reverts the heightmap-aware clause), this test will FAIL deterministically, calling out the regression by name in its assertion message.

follow_ups: If Agent 03 also lands fixes 4.A / 2.A / 3.A this wave, consider follow-up tests for those invariants in a subsequent Agent 11 round (e.g., a unit test that asserts sync_prefab_building_entity dirty-checks .position / .color before assignment, and one that asserts the dead cave-entrance shader branch is gated by a `cave_hole_radius > 0.0` guard). Not in scope for this wave; PM to dispatch separately if desired.

Pre-fix FAIL was demonstrated via in-process monkey-patch harness rather than a clean revert + re-run. If the PM wants a hardcopy pytest FAIL artifact for the audit trail, the simulation script in commands_run[2] can be saved as tests/_manual/wk58_w6_fog_gate_bug_repro.py (in a manual / non-pytest dir to avoid collecting it) so it can be re-run on demand without touching game/ code.
